title: Correlation-driven phonon renormalisation and the equation of state of $γ$-cerium
authors: Yao Wei, Siyu Chen, Evgeny Plekhanov, Ivan Stich, Cedric Weber, Jan M. Tomczak
abstract: We investigate the thermodynamic properties of elemental cerium by assessing the crucial role of phonon free energy within the framework of dynamical mean-field theory (DMFT). While conventional density functional theory (DFT) often fails to capture the intricate energy landscape of $f$-electron materials, our approach integrates many-body electronic correlations with lattice dynamics to achieve a more rigorous description of the equation of state. We calculate the total energy as a function of the lattice constant at both the DFT and DFT+DMFT levels, subsequently incorporating the vibrational free energy derived from the phonon density of states. Our findings reveal that electronic renormalisation of the force constants significantly alters the phonon spectra, particularly in the strongly correlated $γ$-phase. By applying these phonon corrections to the energy profiles, we observe a substantial refinement in the predicted equilibrium volumes. Using principal-component-based machine learning, we interpolate phonon dispersions continuously from a finite set of first-principles calculations and compare them to experiment, finding significantly closer agreement compared to conventional DFT and DFT+U calculations that neglect dynamical many-body correlations. This study underlines the necessity of accounting for both electronic and vibrational entropy when evaluating the phase stability and structural transitions of lanthanide systems under varying pressures and temperatures.
summary: This work develops a comprehensive theoretical framework to calculate the equation of state of cerium by coupling Dynamical Mean-Field Theory (DMFT) with phonon calculations. By treating both electronic correlations and vibrational entropy simultaneously, the authors achieve significant quantitative improvements over standard DFT methods. This highlights that for strongly correlated lanthanides, accurate phase stability requires accounting for both electronic renormalization and lattice vibrations.
main_problem: To rigorously determine the equation of state and thermodynamic properties of elemental cerium, specifically quantifying the role of phonon free energy renormalized by strong electronic correlations.
main_result: The inclusion of both DMFT-derived electronic correlations and vibrational entropy significantly refines the predicted equilibrium volume, leading to better agreement with experimental measurements than standard DFT or DFT+U methods.
method: The study combines Dynamical Mean-Field Theory (DMFT) calculations for electronic structure with lattice dynamics calculations that incorporate phonon free energy corrections across varying lattice constants.
model_or_system: The system is elemental Cerium (Ce), focusing on the $\gamma$-phase, which exhibits strong $4f$ electron correlations. The theoretical framework extends DFT by incorporating DMFT to handle local electronic correlation effects ($U$) and adding vibrational entropy corrections derived from phonon calculations.
key_observables: Total energy vs. lattice constant, phonon spectra/dispersions, equilibrium volume ($a_0$), and the total free energy $F(T)$.
important_parameters: Hubbard interaction strength ($U=6.0 	ext{ eV}$), inverse temperature ($ eta = 20 	ext{ eV}^{-1}$, corresponding to $\sim 580 	ext{ K}$).
main_assumptions: The fixed self-energy approximation is used for phonon calculations, neglecting the explicit displacement dependence of the local self-energy. The harmonic approximation is used for vibrational free energy.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate lattice parameter dependence of total energy (DFT vs DMFT), correlation-driven phonon dispersions showing volume dependence, and temperature/lattice constant dependence of the full free energy functional.
paper_structure: The paper systematically builds the thermodynamic description: first establishing the failure of standard DFT for $f$-electron materials; second, incorporating electronic correlations via DMFT to refine the internal energy; third, adding vibrational entropy corrections derived from phonon calculations; and finally, combining all terms to yield a temperature-dependent equation of state.
why_interesting: The methodology—combining advanced many-body theory (DMFT) with lattice dynamics and finite-temperature thermodynamics—is highly relevant for understanding strongly correlated materials where electronic structure dictates vibrational behavior, which is a key area in modern condensed matter physics.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 2. Ab initio computation of correlation-driven phonon dispersions in cerium. (a, c) Full phonon dispersion relations explicitly calculated from first principles within standard DFT (LDA, T = 0) and DFT+DMFT (T = 580 K), using a 4 × 4 × 4 q-point mesh. The colour bar indicates the lattice constant (in ˚A), with dashed lines marking the respective theoretical equilibrium constants (aDFT 0 = 4.52 ˚A; aDMFT 0 = 4.88 ˚A) from Fig.1.(b, d) Detailed views of the directly computed large- volume γ-phase regime (a = 5.05, 5.15, and 5.25 ˚A)

FIG. 3. Phonon dispersion relations obtained using principal component-based neural network interpolation trained on LDA+DMFT calculations, compared with experimental data from cerium under hydrostatic pressure at ambient tempera- ture from Ref.20. Results are shown for lattice constants a=5.105, 5.120 and 5.160˚A along the Γ–X–K–Γ high-symmetry path. The predicted dispersions (green lines) show very good agreement with the experimental phonon frequencies (black symbols).

FIG. 4. Average phonon frequency as a function of lattice constant for elemental cerium, calculated within DFT (blue squares) and DFT+DMFT (red squares). The shaded region represents the difference in the average phonon frequency be- tween the two approaches, defined as ∆ω = ωLDA+DMFT avg − ωLDA avg . In the compressed volume regime corresponding to the α-phase, the two methods yield nearly identical average frequencies, indicating a minor influence of electronic correla- tions on lattice dynamics. Upon lattice expansion towards the γ-phase, a systematic deviation develops, with LDA+DMFT suppressing the volume-induced phonon softening observed at the LDA level.

FIG. 5. Temperature dependence of the absolute phonon free energy, Fph, of elemental cerium calculated within (a) LDA and (b) LDA+DMFT for lattice constants between 4.30 and 5.25 ˚A. The LDA+DMFT phonon dispersions are computed at β = 20 (T ≈580 K) and used within the harmonic approx- imation.

Figure 4 demonstrates a pronounced correlation- dependent modification of the lattice dynamics across the α–γ transition of the average phonon frequency. The av- erage is obtained from the full phonon dispersions calcu- lated within LDA and LDA+DMFT, shown in Fig. 2 ((a) and (c) panels, respectively), by averaging the phonon frequencies along the chosen high symmetry path in the Brillouin zone. In the compressed volume regime corre- sponding to the α-phase, the two approaches yield nearly identical average frequencies, such that ∆ωavg remains close to zero.
